creator: jhibbits@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Chattrapat Sangmanee from comment #53)

I don't think tbsync acts as a "band-aid", because the whole purpose of the tbsync is to synchronize the timebases between processors.  Once they're synchronized, everything should be fine.  Since everything is fine with the tbsync fix, then it's apparent that the CPUs are not, in fact, correctly synchronized.
